name: Borderlands and Alcorlo Reservoir Loop
name_original: Cogolludo y Jadraque desde Espinosa de Henares — circular por el Embalse de Alcorlo
description: This difficult 34.8-mile touring cycling route from Espinosa de Henares offers scenic views of Alcorlo Reservoir and historic towns.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Espinosa De Henares offers diverse landscapes and quiet roads, making it an excellent destination for outdoor activities. The region, part of "La Alcarria Alta," features open landscapes, gentle slopes, and numerous short climbs, suitable for various fitness levels. Cyclists can traverse picturesque fields and expansive dehesas, with the Henares River and its ancient bridge adding to the scenic appeal. The area provides a blend of natural beauty and historical charm for those exploring by bike. Best touring cycling routes around Espinosa De Henares The most popular touring cycling route is Álvar Fáñez Route and Brihuega Loop, a 34.2 miles (55.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route connects historical towns and offers varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Mandayona and Ledanca from Jadraque — circular by the Camino del Cid, a difficult 37.3 miles (60.0 km) path. It features open landscapes and views of the Henares River valley. Local touring cyclists also love the Jadraque Borderlands Loop, a 15.3 miles (24.6 km) trail leading through open countryside and gentle hills, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Touring cycling around Espinosa De Henares is defined by open landscapes, quiet roads, and the Henares River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 70 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Espinosa De Henares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Espinosa de Henares?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes available around Espinosa de Henares on komoot, offering diverse landscapes and experiences for cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in this region?
A: The region, part of "La Alcarria Alta," features open landscapes, gentle slopes, and numerous short climbs. You'll traverse picturesque fields of lavender and expansive dehesas, with quiet roads ideal for cycling. The Henares River valley also offers scenic views.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes?
A: While many routes are classified as difficult, there are options suitable for varying fitness levels. For a moderate ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188060077/jadraque-borderlands-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jadraque Borderlands Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 24.6 km long and leads through open countryside and gentle hills. The area's quiet roads generally make it pleasant for less experienced cyclists.

Q: What historical sites or natural landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in history and natural beauty. You can explore the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hita&lt;/a&gt;, known for its medieval old town, or visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jadraque Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The Henares River itself is a significant natural feature, with an ancient bridge in Espinosa de Henares. Further along, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5855101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcorlo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185572381/alvar-fanez-route-and-brihuega-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Álvar Fáñez Route and Brihuega Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 55.1 km circular trail connecting historical towns. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188076444/mandayona-and-ledanca-from-jadraque-circular-by-the-camino-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mandayona and Ledanca from Jadraque — circular by the Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt;, which offers open landscapes and views of the Henares River valley.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Espinosa de Henares?
A: The region is characterized by open landscapes, suggesting spring and autumn would be ideal for touring cycling. During these seasons, temperatures are generally milder, and the natural scenery, including fields of lavender, is at its most picturesque, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

Q: Can I bring my dog on these touring cycling routes?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on cycling routes are not detailed, the open landscapes and quiet roads around Espinosa de Henares are generally conducive to cycling with dogs, provided they are well-behaved and kept under control. Always ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and terrain, and carry enough water for both of you.

Q: How can I get to Espinosa de Henares with my bike using public transport?
A: Espinosa de Henares is accessible by public transport. For specific information on bringing bikes on trains (Renfe) or buses to the Guadalajara province, it's best to check the respective transport provider's official website for their current policies and schedules, as these can vary.

Q: Where can I park my car when cycling in Espinosa de Henares?
A: Espinosa de Henares is a small town, and parking facilities are generally available within the town itself. Given its rural setting, you should be able to find suitable parking for your vehicle, especially near the start points of many routes or in the town center.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, Espinosa de Henares and surrounding villages offer amenities for cyclists. Accommodations like "Casas de Valois" cater specifically to cyclists, providing rural apartments. You can find cafes, pubs, and restaurants in the town and nearby historic villages like Hita, perfect for a break or an overnight stay.

Q: What makes the cycling around Espinosa de Henares unique?
A: The uniqueness of cycling around Espinosa de Henares lies in its compelling blend of historical charm and natural beauty. You'll experience tranquil, low-traffic roads, varied terrain with gentle climbs, and open vistas, all set within a landscape of riverine beauty and agricultural charm, with proximity to historically rich towns like Hita.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, the region offers several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185667315/jadraque-y-hita-desde-carrascosa-de-henares-circular-a-los-valles-y-castillos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jadraque y Hita desde Carrascosa de Henares — circular a los Valles y Castillos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 57.7 km route with significant elevation gain, perfect for experienced riders seeking a longer adventure through valleys and past castles.

Q: Can I find routes that are part of larger regional networks like the Camino del Cid?
A: Yes, the region connects to broader networks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188076444/mandayona-and-ledanca-from-jadraque-circular-by-the-camino-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mandayona and Ledanca from Jadraque — circular by the Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that incorporates a section of the famous Camino del Cid, allowing you to experience a part of this historic trail.
tips: 10th century bridge over the Henares River created during the Caliphate of Guadalajara for the passage of trade to Zaragoza. Today the river landscape can provide you with shade and a place to rest on your journey.

Cogolludo is a small town on top of a hill with panoramic views of the terraces of Vega del Henares. You must visit the Renaissance Doge's Palace from the 15th century. The ornate ceilings are amazing.
Pedaling along the southern shore of the Alcorlo reservoir, you will reach the dam's viewpoint to have incredible views of the power of water. The reservoir is near San Andrés del Congosto.

Hard to miss in Jadraque: the fortress high up on the castle hill, also called "Castillo del Cid". Its current appearance can be traced back to the 15th century, but it was probably already there as a somewhat smaller fortress during the times of the caliphate.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Cogolludo y Jadraque circular touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging touring cycling route typically takes around 3 hours and 53 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level, pace, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or visit points of interest.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on mostly paved surfaces, making it suitable for touring or road bikes. Given its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation gain, be prepared for some challenging ascents and descents. While mostly paved, some sections might include unpaved or more demanding terrain.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as a 'difficult bike ride' and requires very good fitness due to its length of nearly 56 kilometers and an elevation gain of almost 700 meters. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a mix of natural beauty and historical sites. You'll pass by the picturesque Alcorlo Reservoir, the historic town of Jadraque (known for its castle), and the charming town of Cogolludo, home to the impressive Ducal Palace of Cogolludo. You'll also cross the Stone Bridge over the Henares River.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Espinosa de Henares. You should be able to find parking within the town, typically near the main square or public areas, to begin your touring cycling adventure.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring or autumn months. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, making the significant distance and elevation gain more enjoyable. Summers can be very hot in this region of Spain, and winters can be cold with a higher chance of adverse weather.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this touring route. It primarily uses public roads and paths through the region.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While cycling routes themselves don't typically have dog restrictions, it's important to consider the nature of this particular route. It's a long and difficult cycling trail, which might not be suitable for all dogs, especially if they need to run alongside for extended periods. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are well-trained, can handle the distance, and are kept on a leash in populated areas or near wildlife.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this touring cycling route overlap with several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Tierras de Frontera, the Camino del Cid (Senderista), and the GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through towns like Cogolludo and Jadraque, you will find opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel and refresh. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the longer stretches between towns.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given that the route is primarily on mostly paved surfaces but is also long and challenging, a touring bicycle or a robust road bike with comfortable gearing would be ideal. Ensure your bike is in good condition and suitable for significant elevation changes.

waypoints.name: Stone Bridge over the Henares River
